recommendations: Add a minimal smoke-test suite for the MCP plugin (server startup
tool listing
one call per endpoint)
Wire MCP tests into CI (`.github/workflows/tests.yml`) with a matrix for Python 3.9–3.12 and optional deps toggles (onnx/tensorflow extras)
Define strict Pydantic request/response schemas per endpoint (especially `aizynthfinder`
`configuration`
`mctssearchtree`
`treeanalysis`) with explicit validation errors
Add integration tests that execute a tiny end-to-end retrosynthesis run from fixture config through `TreeAnalysis` and assert deterministic output shape
Improve dependency packaging by exposing extras in `pyproject.toml` (e.g. `mcp`
`ml-tf`
`mongo`
`gui`) and keeping `mcp_output/requirements.txt` synchronized or generated
Add graceful degradation paths and clear messages when optional backends (TensorFlow/Keras
ONNXRuntime
RDKit-related features) are unavailable
Introduce structured logging and correlation IDs across MCP calls plus configurable log level via env vars
Add timeout/cancellation controls and resource guards for search-heavy endpoints to prevent runaway MCTS jobs
Cache immutable heavy artifacts (models/config/stock handles) in the adapter layer to reduce repeated initialization overhead
Add endpoint-level auth/rate-limit hooks (even if optional) for safer remote deployment
Expand `README_MCP.md` with concrete examples (local run
sample JSON-RPC/tool payloads
troubleshooting missing models/data)
Add contract tests to ensure endpoint names and signatures remain stable across releases
Pin and document supported aizynthfinder version ranges for the MCP plugin to avoid API drift
Add basic benchmarking scripts for latency/memory of key endpoints (`aizynthfinder`
`mctssearchtree`
`treeanalysis`) and track regressions in CI
Improve error taxonomy by mapping domain exceptions to consistent MCP error codes/messages
Validate plugin import mode fallback to CLI mode in tests for environments where direct imports fail
Add pre-commit hooks (ruff/black/isort/mypy) and enforce them in CI for both source and `mcp_output`
Generate API docs for exposed MCP tools from schema/docstrings to keep docs aligned with implementation

success_factors: Workflow completed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize) with overall status=success.
Low-intrusiveness import strategy was feasible (import feasibility 0.86), enabling adapter_mode=import.
Repository structure was analyzable despite SCM fallback; zip_fallback still produced full file inventory and module map.
MCP plugin artifacts were generated coherently (entrypoint, adapter, service, requirements, README) and service health reported healthy.
failure_reasons: Quality-validation gap: original project tests were not executed successfully (passed=false, execution_time=0), so compatibility confidence is incomplete.
Observability gap: node-level timings, resource metrics, and plugin LOC/size are effectively missing (0/unknown), limiting diagnostic fidelity.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external semantic enrichment for architecture/behavior validation.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact duration unavailable. Zip fallback import indicates resilient but potentially slower retrieval path.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with medium complexity assessment and dependency/entry-point extraction; exact timing unavailable.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; 7 MCP files produced and endpoint surface identified. Exact generation duration unavailable.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin health check passed quickly (startup_time=0 reported), but no substantive endpoint/contract/integration test execution captured.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing telemetry (reported 0 MB). Needs real measurement instrumentation.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate expected (small generated plugin footprint), but generated_files_size=0 indicates metrics collection issue.
